##NAMES=Hydrocerussite
##RRUFFID=R160062
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Pb_3_(CO_3_)_2_(OH)_2_
##LOCALITY=Tiger, Pinal County, Arizona, USA
##OWNER=University of Arizona Mineral Museum
##SOURCE=University of Arizona Mineral Museum
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless massive crystals, associated with pale bluish leadhillite and colorless susannite. Both leadhillite and susannite show good cleavage.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R160062
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.2523 b: 5.2523 c: 24.0002 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 573.39 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
